title: Almond financiers
subtitle: Small French brown butter and almond cakes
excerpt: Small French cakes made with almond flour, browned butter, and egg whites. Crisp on the outside, tender and moist inside. Baked in financier moulds or a mini muffin tin. Makes twelve.
sourceNotes: Financiers are a Parisian bakery tradition, documented in French household baking manuals from the nineteenth century. The name is associated with the Bourse district of Paris, where the small rectangular cakes were sold to financiers who could eat them without marking their suits. The original recipe uses beurre noisette, almond flour, icing sugar, and egg whites in a ratio documented in French pâtisserie manuals predating the twentieth century.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze fully cooled. Eat from frozen or thaw at room temperature for 30 minutes.
recipe.batchNotes: Doubles cleanly. Rest the second batch of batter while the first bakes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The batter can be made and refrigerated for up to 24 hours before baking.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; 200°C in a conventional oven

glossaryTerms.definition: Brown butter — unsalted butter cooked over medium heat until the milk solids on the base turn deep amber and smell of toasted hazelnuts. The French name means hazelnut butter, referring to the colour and flavour rather than any actual nuts. The process takes about 5 minutes and is stopped by pouring the butter into a cool bowl; left in the hot pan, it continues to cook toward burnt.

content.text: Financiers are one of the most reliable small cakes in the baker's repertoire: quick to make, forgiving in the tin, and better from the fridge as batter than freshly mixed. The
is the defining flavour. Brown it properly and cool it before mixing; adding hot butter to the egg whites will cook them.
Brown the butter and make the batter

Melt the {{unsalted-butter}} in a small saucepan over medium heat. Continue cooking until the solids on the base turn deep amber and smell nutty, about 5 minutes. Pour into a bowl and cool to room temperature.

Whisk together the {{almond-flour}}, {{icing-sugar}}, and {{plain-flour}} in a large bowl. Stir in the egg whites from {{eggs}} and {{vanilla-extract}} until smooth. Pour in the cooled brown butter and mix until fully incorporated. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Preheat the oven to 190°C fan. Butter a 12-hole mini muffin tin generously. Fill each hole three-quarters full with batter. Bake for 12–14 minutes, until the tops are deep gold at the edges and set in the centre when pressed lightly. Cool in the tin for 3 minutes, then ease out with a small palette knife.
